title: Read what Bruce Springsteen had to say about Trump, ICE, and America in Boston Sunday
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized President Trump and his administration during his Boston concert at TD Garden on Sunday night.
Springsteen addressed issues like the erosion of American values, illegal immigration detentions, and threats to democracy and civil rights.
The concert was part of Springsteen's "Land of Hope and Dreams Tour" and included tributes to victims of violence and calls for unity.

title: Review and setlist: Springsteen eviscerates Trump at fiery TD Garden stop
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen delivered a politically charged performance at Boston's TD Garden, emphasizing themes of democracy and civic responsibility.
The setlist included 27 songs, with 'War', 'Born in the USA', and 'Streets of Minneapolis' highlighting contemporary issues.
Guest guitarist Tom Morello joined the E Street Band, enhancing the concert's energetic and urgent performances.

title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, celebrating both American history and music.
The concert featured American classics like Dudley Buck’s 'Festival Overture on The Star Spangled Banner' and Duke Ellington’s 'It Don’t Mean a Thing.'
Odom Jr. performed hits including 'Fly Me To The Moon' and 'Alexander Hamilton,' showcasing his Broadway and soulful influences.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, faces 12 charges related to violent acts against two children under 14 in Chelsea.
Bautista-Zepeda allegedly burned children with a lighter and heated fork, and recorded violently assaulting one child.
Authorities arrested Bautista-Zepeda in Virginia; he was held without bail pending a dangerousness hearing on May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, from Concord, was convicted of rape and indecent assault by a Barnstable County court.
The crimes occurred in July 2023 at a Sandwich restaurant, with the victim escaping after an assault in the restroom.
Jammal received a 13.5 to 15-year prison sentence plus five years probation for the assault conviction.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's Church of the Open Word will be converted into condominiums after city officials approved the redevelopment plan.
The project includes preserving the church sanctuary for six condos and building a five-story structure with 26 additional units.
The church, previously affiliated with the Swedenborgian Church of North America, closed in 2020 and was sold in 2024.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard is a potential trade target for the Celtics, providing versatility and championship experience.
LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, could boost the Celtics' lineup but is a long-shot due to personal ties in LA.
John Collins could enhance forward depth for the Celtics, after averaging 13 points and 5 rebounds last season.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advances to NCAA Division II World Series after winning Super Regional against Molloy University.
Endicott College secures fourth consecutive Division III championship appearance after defeating Salve Regina University.
Bentley and Endicott compete in North Carolina and Ohio respectively; opponents and schedules are TBD.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics plan to work out UConn forward Alex Karaban next week ahead of NBA Draft.
Karaban is ranked No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 and won two NCAA championships with UConn.
Karaban finished his college career as UConn's winningest player with 126 victories and all-time leader in several statistical categories.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth's 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour' stopped at MGM Music Hall, blending jazz and pop influences.
Puth graduated from Berklee College of Music and shared nostalgic Boston anecdotes during his performance.
Opening acts Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey showcased indie-pop vocals and live looping performances, respectively.
